description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a distinct, textured appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid black, contrasting with the speckled, granite-like texture of the top. The block appears robust and heavy, likely made from a dense stone material such as granite or basalt. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly for controlling pedestrian or vehicular movement. The overall finish is rough and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or engravings.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with a grooved, speckled top and smooth black sides, designed as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved, speckled top surface and solid black side face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the parallel grooves on the top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the grooved top and solid black side visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, evenly spaced grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a pedestrian barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
